title: US plane went to Middle East after Prestwick Airport stop

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: 'Clear answers' have been demanded after a U.S military plane stopped at Prestwick Airport on the way to the Middle East amid the ongoing war with Iran . An Air Force transport plane bound for a base in southern Turkey stopped in South Ayrshire en route, with the publicly owned transport hub frequently used for military activities. On Saturday the United States and Israel launched a long-expected offensive against Iran, with American forces having been massing in the gulf for several weeks.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The strikes have been described as illegal under international law by French President Emmanuel Macron and Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, the latter of whom has banned American military planes from using Spanish bases. Clarity sought on whether aircraft used in Iran stopped in Scotland Scottish Government in contact with Westminster after strikes on Iran The Scottish Government said on Wednesday it had asked the UK Government for clarification over whether any aircraft which had used Scottish airports are involved in the operation.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: On March 3 a Boeing C-17A Globemaster III, 04-4131, took off from Prestwick Airport at 8.43am, heading to Greece and then Turkey.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – US military plane uses Prestwick Airport before flying to the Middle East
